title: The Anthropic IPO Could Make These 5 AI Stocks Unexpected Winners
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: Anthropic's IPO filing at a ~$1 trillion valuation will drive massive AI infrastructure spending. The company has committed $100+ billion to AWS and Google for compute capacity, positioning infrastructure suppliers like Celestica, Credo Technology, Astera Labs, Coherent, and Marvell Technology to benefit from the resulting data center build-out.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Integrates GPUs and networking gear into AI racks for hyperscalers; positioned as primary assembler for Anthropic's infrastructure expansion with recent product launches.
Supplies Active Electrical Cables for AI clusters; has three confirmed hyperscaler customers with direct Anthropic relationships, creating structural demand linkage.
Designs critical semiconductor connectivity silicon for AI racks; recently launched advanced fabric switch now shipping to leading cloud customers supporting infrastructure expansion.
Provides optical transceivers connecting data center servers; backed by $2 billion Nvidia investment and multibillion-dollar purchase commitment, positioned for optical demand growth.
Primary architect of custom silicon for hyperscalers; Nvidia CEO identified as potential trillion-dollar company; stock surged 33% on single day with strong design win pipeline.
Holds substantial equity stake in Anthropic and secured $100B+ compute commitment; positioned to benefit from infrastructure spending through AWS services and Trainium accelerators.
Holds substantial equity stake in Anthropic and secured 5 gigawatts of compute capacity commitment, positioning Google Cloud for significant infrastructure revenue.
Holds substantial equity stake in Anthropic and secured 5 gigawatts of compute capacity commitment, positioning Google Cloud for significant infrastructure revenue.

title: Meta’s AI Monetization Model Sets the Standard for Hyperscaler Capex
author: Opeyemi Babalola
description: Meta's AI-driven ad ranking delivers four times the revenue impact of increased ad load, with Q4 2025 ad revenue up 24% YoY. The company's incremental return on invested capital for AI investments exceeds 20%, validating aggressive capex expansion to $125-145B in 2026. Google's $80B additional AI investment announcement benefits TPU supply chain partners including Broadcom, Celestica, and Lumentum. The broader hyperscaler cohort is projected to spend over $600B on infrastructure in 2026, with 75% targeting AI specifically.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Demonstrated strong AI monetization with 24% YoY ad revenue growth, 4x revenue impact from AI-driven ad ranking, and incremental ROIC above 20%. Significantly increased capex guidance to $125-145B for 2026, validating confidence in AI deployment returns.

Announced additional $80B AI investment with capex budget projected at $175-185B for 2026. Google Cloud operating income surged 85% with margins expanding to 23.7%, demonstrating ability to self-fund infrastructure build and compete on both consumer and enterprise sides.

Announced additional $80B AI investment with capex budget projected at $175-185B for 2026. Google Cloud operating income surged 85% with margins expanding to 23.7%, demonstrating ability to self-fund infrastructure build and compete on both consumer and enterprise sides.

AI semiconductor revenue reached $8.4B in Q1 FY2026 (up 106% YoY) with guidance of $10.7B for Q2. Secured long-term TPU supply agreement with Google through 2031, with TPU-related shipments accounting for ~78% of ASIC revenue.
Handles system-level board and rack assembly for Google's AI server deployments. Benefits directly from Google's escalating capex commitment and TPU supply chain expansion.

Supplies optical transceivers and components for Google's datacenter-scale cluster systems. Positioned to benefit from increased infrastructure spending as hyperscalers expand AI data center capacity.

CEO Jensen Huang validated Meta's AI deployment efficiency and endorsed the broader capex cycle. Noted that agentic AI compute requirements have risen 1,000% compared to generative AI, suggesting sustained demand for infrastructure investment.

title: What to Know About This Fund's $74.8 Million Indivior Sale
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Divisadero Street Capital Management sold 2.26 million shares of Indivior (INDV) for approximately $74.76 million in Q1 2026, reducing its stake to 521,083 shares. The sale appears to be profit-taking after the stock surged nearly 200% over the past year. Despite the fund's exit, Indivior's fundamentals remain strong with Q1 revenue up 19% year-over-year to $317 million and SUBLOCADE sales jumping 32% to $232 million, prompting management to raise 2026 guidance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite the fund's partial exit, Indivior demonstrates strong business momentum with 19% revenue growth, 32% SUBLOCADE sales growth, doubled adjusted EBITDA to $164 million, and raised 2026 guidance to $1.29 billion in revenue. The sale is characterized as profit-taking rather than a sign of business deterioration, and the company's underlying fundamentals remain intact with over 500,000 patients on SUBLOCADE.
Mentioned as a top holding of Divisadero Street Capital Management ($86.14 million, 4.0% of AUM) but no specific news or analysis provided about the company itself.
Listed as a top holding of Divisadero Street Capital Management ($89.95 million, 4.2% of AUM) but no specific news or analysis provided about the company itself.

title: Canadian General Investments: Investment Update - Unaudited
author: Canadian General Investments, Limited
description: Canadian General Investments (CGI) reported a NAV of $86.82 per share at April 30, 2026, with year-to-date and 12-month NAV returns of 8.0% and 42.3% respectively, outperforming the S&P/TSX Composite Index. The company's leverage increased to 12.4% of net assets, and its portfolio is diversified across sectors with top holdings including Celestica, NVIDIA, and Franco-Nevada.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Largest holding in CGI's portfolio at 6.0%, indicating confidence in the company's prospects
Co-second-largest holding at 4.5%, indicating significant portfolio allocation to precious metals and mining sector

title: Why Celestica Stock Is Plummeting Today
author: Keith Noonan
description: Celestica stock plunged 15.7% despite beating Q1 earnings expectations and raising full-year guidance. The company reported $4.05B in revenue (53% YoY growth) and $2.16 adjusted EPS, exceeding analyst forecasts, while raising annual sales guidance to $19B and EPS guidance to $10.15. The sharp sell-off suggests a valuation pullback despite strong fundamentals.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock fell 15.37% despite beating earnings expectations and raising full-year guidance significantly. The disconnect between strong financial results and sharp stock decline indicates investor concerns about valuation or market conditions outweighing positive fundamentals.
